findings.description: search_by_name builds the LIKE clause via f-string interpolation of `fragment`, allowing SQL injection.
reserve uses `stock > quantity` instead of `>=`, so an exact-stock reservation is wrongly rejected (off-by-one).
Check-then-update in reserve is not atomic (TOCTOU), so concurrent reservations can oversell and drive stock negative.
reserve does not validate `quantity`; a negative value passes the guard and increases stock via `stock - (-n)`.
low_stock_report issues a separate COUNT query per low-stock product (N+1) instead of a single aggregate join.
restock does not validate `quantity` (negative reduces stock) and silently no-ops on a nonexistent SKU.
Product(**row) is fragile and will raise TypeError if the SELECT column set ever drifts from the dataclass fields.
A new SQLite connection is opened and closed on every call rather than reused/pooled.
reserve commits per call with no surrounding transaction, so multi-step reserve flows cannot be rolled back atomically.
